seniority.level: Staff / Principal IC (likely PM6 or equivalent)
level_signals: at least 3 years on ML, search, recommendations, or AI-powered products at scale
Ability to earn the respect of Staff and Principal-level engineers and researchers
Comp range $232K-$282K base, consistent with Staff-level PM at Airbnb

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with at least 3 years on ML, search, recommendations, or AI-powered products at scale
at least 3 years on ML, search, recommendations, or AI-powered products at scale
Track record of owning strategy and roadmap for technically complex systems — not just managing features, but setting direction and making hard prioritization calls
Strong experimentation fluency: comfortable designing A/B tests, interpreting counterfactual results, and reasoning about proxy metrics vs. long-term outcomes
Experience working on two-sided marketplaces or platforms where you balanced supply-side and demand-side tradeoffs
Ability to earn the respect of Staff and Principal-level engineers and researchers — you don't need to write code, but you need to engage at a level that builds credibility
Clear, structured communicator who can distill complex technical tradeoffs for executives and translate strategic intent into actionable guidance for engineers

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with LLM-based products in production — ideally involving evaluation challenges, latency constraints, or safety and guardrail work (preferred)
Familiarity with reinforcement learning, contextual bandits, or explore/exploit frameworks in a product context (preferred)
Background working alongside applied researchers or with teams that publish externally (preferred)

explicitly_excluded: Candidates located in: Alaska, Indiana, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, Wisconsin, Alabama, Mississippi, Oklahoma, Delaware, Rhode Island

scope.surface: Relevance & Personalization platform — search ranking, recommendations, personalization, and generative AI across guest and host journeys
primary_metrics_inferred: Bookings driven by relevance and personalization improvements
core_responsibilities: Define and drive the roadmap for Airbnb's relevance and personalization platform — from natural language query understanding to multi-turn, context-aware discovery experiences
Make prioritization calls that balance multiple competing objectives: guest experience, host success, revenue, fairness, and marketplace health
Partner with ML engineers and applied researchers to shape model strategy, evaluation frameworks, and experimentation design
Align cross-functional partners — Guest, Host, MarTech, Trust & Safety, Customer Support — on shared goals and sequencing
Drive the Tripcycle vision: ensuring R&P's intelligence layer connects across the full trip lifecycle, from inspiration through post-trip
Design and oversee A/B experiments with rigorous metric design and long-term effect measurement
Translate complex technical tradeoffs into clear decisions for engineering teams and concise narratives for leadership
Stay close to guests and hosts: synthesize user research, marketplace data, and competitive signals to sharpen your intuition and refine strategy

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as potential additional component alongside bonus and Employee Travel Credits

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($232K-$282K base)
Clearly defined product surface with strategic ownership (not a feature-level PM role)
Team described in detail: ML engineers, applied researchers, and technical program managers
Long-term, ambitious vision (Tripcycle, travel companion) signals strategic investment
Honest about preferred vs. required criteria — three explicit 'preferred' signals
red_flags: Role requires physical presence in San Francisco, CA — no hybrid or remote flexibility stated
Large exclusion list of US states where employees cannot be located
10+ years PM requirement with 3+ years ML/AI is a high bar that narrows the candidate pool significantly
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear for international candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + 10+ years requirement + Staff/Principal-level collaboration signal)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
posting_first_seen and posting_last_updated (not available in provided data)
ambiguities: Location type: JD says 'the successful applicant will need to be based in San Francisco, CA' suggesting onsite, but does not explicitly state whether hybrid options exist. Treated as onsite given mandatory SF presence language.

The phrase 'This role may also be eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits' uses 'may also be eligible', making equity non-guaranteed; captured as equity_mentioned: true with qualifier.
